symptoms.symptom_name: Explosive behavioural escalation timed to a loud sensory stimulus

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sensory-triggered meltdown in autism spectrum disorder
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tight temporal link between an aversive auditory stimulus and escalation in a child with sensory hypersensitivity.
An external, mechanistic trigger argues against an internally driven psychotic process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained violent aggression toward caregivers and self-injurious glass-smashing attempts

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Behavioural emergency with imminent physical-harm risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Self-directed and other-directed violence with a lethal means (glass) present defines the emergency and drives the triage tier.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Behavioural emergency with imminent physical-harm risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elopement into traffic is an immediate life-threat requiring emergency-services dispatch.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-verbal in crisis, cannot be verbally de-escalated

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to self-report or be reasoned with removes de-escalation levers and hides a possible medical driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known autism spectrum disorder with sensory hypersensitivity and repetitive behaviours

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sensory-triggered meltdown in autism spectrum disorder
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The established diagnosis and sensory profile provide the mechanism for the meltdown.

symptoms.symptom_name: No volunteered localising features of physical illness (not holding ears/abdomen), unexamined

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of volunteered localising signs is reassuring but weak in a non-verbal child; does not exclude an occult driver.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Behavioural emergency with imminent physical-harm risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Evidence that self-injury is already occurring; video-observable and lay-reportable, so admissible in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 135), hypertension (BP 145/95), tachypnoea (RR 28), temperature 37.0, SpO2 99% — clinician-measured

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective autonomic arousal consistent with an extreme distress state; measured in person and sealed — the AI infers distress from lay observation, not a measured vital sign. A normal-range temperature does not exclude an occult infective driver.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Structured screen for an occult medical driver once the child is safe (dental, ENT/otoscopy, abdominal exam/bowel history for constipation, bladder scan for retention, fever/infection screen)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Diagnostic overshadowing safeguard: a non-verbal child with new severe behaviour change must be examined for a treatable medical cause; requires in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Medication review for antipsychotic-related akathisia / adverse effects
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Daily risperidone can contribute to restlessness/agitation; review dose and timing with the prescriber.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine bloods / imaging requested remotely by the AI Doctor
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not obtainable via telehealth and not the AI's role; investigation belongs to the in-person emergency and follow-up teams.
